[[BLOCK:p_3]]
PSYM 5000 Course Syllabus

[[BLOCK:p_4]]
Spring 2026

[[BLOCK:p_6]]
Course Title and Number: PSYM 5000

[[BLOCK:p_7]]
Course Title: Introduction to Psychology (心理学导论)  
Semester: Spring 2026

[[BLOCK:p_8]]
Course Format: Asynchronous online course (asynchronous, online)

[[BLOCK:p_10]]
Name: Hui Zhang

[[BLOCK:p_11]]
Location: Online Instruction

[[BLOCK:p_13]]
This course is taught by Professor Hui Zhang, who has more than 20 years of experience delivering public lectures in psychology, providing psychological counseling services, and offering professional skills training, with a particular emphasis on individual and group counseling. Professor Zhang earned a bachelor’s degree in Preventive Medicine, a master’s degree in Management Psychology, and a Ph.D. in Psychiatry and Mental Health from Capital Medical University (首都医科大学), building a strong interdisciplinary foundation that bridges medicine and psychology.

Professor Zhang has long taught core undergraduate and graduate courses, including Medical Psychology and Mental Health, and previously served as the lead instructor in developing a nationally recognized high-quality open online course. Her teaching excellence has been widely acknowledged, including receiving the Excellence Award in the National Medical Humanities Course Teaching Competition for Higher Medical Institutions. As a principal investigator or key collaborator, she has led or contributed to multiple national- and provincial-level research projects. She has published numerous articles in leading academic journals, many of which are indexed in the Science Citation Index (SCI) and the Social Sciences Citation Index (SSCI). She has also served as chief editor or contributing editor for several scholarly works and nationally designated textbooks, including *Emotional Intelligence Development and Stress Management* and *Practical Medical Psychology*.

[[BLOCK:p_15]]
Course Description:

[[BLOCK:p_16]]
This course provides a comprehensive introduction to psychology and is designed for graduate students who are new to the field or returning after previous study. It examines the fundamental processes underlying behavior, cognition, and emotion, along with their applications in everyday life and specialized settings.
